title: Decode Ways
pattern: Dynamic Programming â€” Fibonacci-style with Validity Checks
coreIdea: Count the number of ways to decode a digit string into letters (where 'A'=1 ... 'Z'=26), using a DP recurrence similar to climbing stairs, but with extra validity checks for single-digit and two-digit decodings (e.g., '0' alone is invalid, two-digit must be between 10-26).
recognitionCues: asks to count total valid decodings
requires careful handling of '0' which cannot stand alone
approach: dp[i] represents ways to decode the first i characters. dp[0]=1. For each position i, if the single digit at i-1 is nonzero (1-9), add dp[i-1]. If the two-digit number formed by positions i-2,i-1 is between 10-26, add dp[i-2]. Sum both contributions for dp[i].
